summary: PR #2543 decompiled grCastle_801CE19C by replacing small MWERKS inline-asm bit manipulation with C, backed by a grCastle_GroundVars9 bitfield for gp+DE bit 0. The same xDE_b0 field was then used in nearby grCastle code to set, clear, and test the flag. The PR also adjusted PAD_STACK usage and kept offset-style naming rather than accepting speculative semantic names.

key_files.role: Replaced xDE raw masks, temporary register variables, and rlwimi inline asm with gp->gv.castle9.xDE_b0 boolean bitfield accesses in grCastle_801CD658, grCastle_801CDFD8, grCastle_801CE19C, and grCastle_801CE578; decompiled grCastle_801CE19C body uses GET_GROUND, a timer decrement, grCastle_801CD4D0, and clears the flag.
Changed grCastle_GroundVars9 field at gp+DE from u8 xDE to a one-bit bitfield named xDE_b0, preserving surrounding offset comments and layout.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:ribbanya issue comment says the struct needed to be modified to use a bitfield

what_changed.text: grCastle_801CE19C was converted from assembly-heavy bit tests/updates on castle9.xDE into C using gp->gv.castle9.xDE_b0, with the countdown timer logic kept explicit.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:PR body says it replaces a small bit of ASM with equivalent C code

what_changed.text: The raw byte field castle9.xDE became a named bitfield xDE_b0 in grCastle_GroundVars9.
Nearby xDE flag set/clear sites were updated from masks, local casted bitfield structs, and rlwimi inline asm to xDE_b0 assignments.
PAD_STACK usage changed from PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(4) in grCastle_801CE19C and a PAD_STACK(4) was added in grCastle_801CE578.

smart_moves.text: Model the flag as a real struct bitfield instead of preserving raw byte masks or inline asm; this made multiple sites simpler and addressed reviewer feedback.
Kept the recovered bitfield name offset-based as xDE_b0 rather than using speculative semantic labels such as armed, pending, or active from the PR body's AI-assisted interpretation.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:PR body marks semantic interpretation as a best guess

decomp_lessons.text: When matching code manipulates a single bit in a known struct byte, try representing the byte as a bitfield in the owning struct; this can remove rlwimi inline asm, register temporaries, raw masks, and local casted bitfield structs.

For decompilation review, mechanical or AI-assisted semantic guesses should not drive names unless corroborated; this PR kept an address/offset-style bitfield name despite a speculative description in the PR body.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:PR body says the functional interpretation was a best guess
raw_diff:field named xDE_b0 rather than a semantic name

naming_conventions.text: Use offset-and-bit names such as xDE_b0 for recovered bitfields when the bit's semantic role is not proven.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:types.h field comment /* +1A:0 gp+DE:0 */ u8 xDE_b0 : 1
human_pr_text:PR body presents armed/pending/active as guesswork

naming_conventions.text: Temporary locals named tmp, byte, one, and zero were removed once the typed bitfield made the operation expressible directly.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:PR body says removing tmp variables seemed necessary

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Replacing rlwimi bit insertion inline asm with a C bitfield assignment matched the intended bit manipulation at xDE_b0.

PAD_STACK remained necessary in the submitted diff, but only narrow evidence is present: PAD_STACK(4) appears in grCastle_801CE19C and grCastle_801CE578. No independent verifier log in the slice explains why the padding was required.
The author reported that nicer-looking variants regressed and that generated bytes matched 100%, but the loaded slice does not include objdiff output specific to grCastle_801CE19C.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report appears to list unrelated gm units rather than grCastle_801CE19C

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Thanks for the contribution. The struct just needed to be modified to use a bitfield. You can see the difference in 545c43ab01894354eb53792742588675a3cf9ad9.

notes: Changed files and code edits are well supported by the raw diff.
Reviewer feedback is available only as an issue comment, with no inline review comments.
The author's 100% byte-match claim is present, but the loaded bot report lists unrelated gm units and does not independently verify grCastle_801CE19C.
The PR body includes AI-assisted semantic guesses about the function's purpose; those are treated as speculation, not accepted naming or behavior facts.
PAD_STACK changes are visible in the diff, but the slice does not include a narrow explanation or verifier evidence for the exact stack-padding requirements.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use owning-struct bitfields to remove raw bit manipulation and inline asm
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2543, changing grCastle_GroundVars9's xDE byte into a bitfield xDE_b0 let the code replace rlwimi inline asm, masks, and local casted bitfield structs with direct boolean field assignments.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer offset-style bitfield names when semantics are speculative
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR retained xDE_b0 instead of adopting speculative names from the PR body's AI-assisted explanation, matching conservative naming guidance when behavior is not proven.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:PR body marks semantic role as best guess

source_update_candidates.title: grCastle_GroundVars9 has a recovered bitfield at gp+DE:0
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2543 changed src/melee/gr/types.h so grCastle_GroundVars9 stores gp+DE bit 0 as `u8 xDE_b0 : 1`, replacing the previous `u8 xDE` byte field and enabling direct accesses in src/melee/gr/grcastle.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source/path-owned struct layout fact visible in the PR diff; curator should decide whether to add it to path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider bitfield recovery before keeping rlwimi asm for single-bit flags
source_update_candidates.text: When a function uses inline asm or raw masks only to set, clear, or test one bit of a known struct byte, check whether the owning struct should expose that bit as a bitfield before preserving the asm.
source_update_candidates.reason: The case reinforces existing standards about typed fields and avoiding inline asm; curator can decide whether it warrants an explicit standard note.

rejection_notes.text: Do not promote the PR body's AI-generated description of grCastle_801CE19C as a one-shot/timer-driven object spawner into accepted behavior or naming facts; the author explicitly framed it as a best guess.
Do not treat the loaded decomp-dev bot report as direct validation of grCastle_801CE19C, because the listed matches/regressions are for gm units rather than the changed grCastle file.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK(4) as a style rule from this PR alone; the diff shows the tactic but does not include verifier evidence explaining it.
